Ad Text:
12 + 12 + 12 month contract – 1st July start date
Baseline Security Clearance - Civic, ACT or Brisbane, QLD
Lead the development, revision and implementation of Information Security Governance processes.

Our client, a Federal Government department based in Civic, ACT that promotes harmonious, productive, cooperative and compliant workplace relations in Australia, requires the services of two Lead Cybersecurity Analysts for 12 + 12 + 12-month contracts.

Duties of the Lead Cybersecurity Analysts:

Lead teams of auditors conducting internal or external audits, producing plans for each audit and solutions and actions with management.
Analyses changes in legislation or regulation and assesses impact.
Provide definitive and expert advice. 
Leads the development, revision or implementation of Information Security Governance processes.

Skills and Experience - Lead Cybersecurity Analysts:

Essential criteria

Capability to conduct evaluations of adherence and compliance with GRC frameworks including PSPF, ISM controls and ACSC Essential 8 in a risk-based approach.
Demonstrated experience providing guidance on security, risk and appropriate procedures / policies to maintain and enhance overall security posture.
Demonstrated technical expertise in delivering periodic assessments to maintain agency’s security posture up to the protected classification level, including the capability to provide Penetration Testing by request.

Technical skills

CISSP/CISM or equivalent certification competencies, IRAP or Essential 8 related auditing experience highly desirable.

Ad Text:
Reporting Manager – Enterprise/Financial Services, TA Reports (Sydney)

As the Reporting Manager, you will join a high-performing Hudson Talent Acquisition team, focusing on supporting one of our clients in the Enterprise/Financial Services sector. Your primary responsibility will be to understand and analyze recruitment processes to enhance information management and support strategic decision-making within the TA teams and client Human Resources functions.

What you’ll do:
 
Operate as a dedicated resource for an enterprise RPO account, ensuring reporting and analytics activities align with client priorities, service level agreements, and overall business objectives.
Oversee data governance and ensure compliance with organizational standards across Avature and BI systems.
Lead the design and implementation of reporting strategies and analytics projects from conception to delivery.
Drive innovation by introducing automation, AI, and Power Platform solutions to optimize reporting processes.
Support the creation of budgets in collaboration with senior stakeholders by providing accurate reporting and financial insights to guide resource allocation and strategic decisions.
Provide leadership and mentoring to the reporting team, fostering a culture of continuous improvement.
Collaborate with senior management on strategic initiatives and present insights to inform decision-making.
Manage stakeholder relationships to ensure reporting aligns with business objectives.

What You Will Bring to the Role:
 
Typically requires at least 5 years of experience in reporting or data analysis roles, with RPO experience strongly preferred.
Proficiency in Excel and BI tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau).
Experience with ATS/CRM/HRIS systems (e.g., Workday, SuccessFactors, Avature, Smart Recruiters), with implementation in this space highly sought after.
Strong financial acumen with the ability to interpret and analyze budget data, ensuring alignment between reporting outputs and financial objectives.
Proven leadership and guidance skills, mentoring junior analysts and fostering a culture of continuous improvement.
Champion innovation by driving the adoption of emerging technologies such as AI, automation, and Microsoft Power Platform (including Power Apps) to optimize reporting strategies and enhance team capabilities.
Demonstrated experience working closely with management or leadership teams, including exposure to decision-making processes and strategic-level reporting.
Experience in managing reporting and analytics projects from conception to implementation.
Excellent relationship management skills, with effective communication ability with stakeholders at all levels of the organization.

What Does Great Look Like in This Role:
 
Proactively identifies challenges and opportunities for improvement, driving innovation in reporting methodologies.
Acts as a trusted advisor for reporting and analytics within the business.
Contributes to the budgeting process to enhance profitability.
Fosters team success through mentoring and knowledge sharing.
Receives consistent positive feedback from stakeholders.
Serves as a proud ambassador of Hudson Talent Solutions.

Senior analytical role within a globally significant Real Assets portfolio
Permanent opportunity located in Melbourne or Sydney

Your New Role

As a Senior Analyst within the Real Assets team, you will support the acquisition and active management of unlisted infrastructure investments across AustralianSuper's global portfolio. The Real Assets portfolio spans major infrastructure sectors including digital infrastructure, energy transition, transport & logistics and social and economic infrastructure.

In this role, you will play a hands-on role in transaction execution and asset management, leading financial modelling workstreams, supporting due diligence, preparing investment papers, and delivering high-quality analysis to inform investment decisions.

You will work closely with senior investment professionals and contribute to portfolio research, valuation, monitoring and reporting activities across direct infrastructure assets and managed funds.

This is a critical role requiring sector expertise, strong analytical capability and the ability to operate effectively in a complex, long-term investment environment.

What You'll Need

To be successful in this role, Senior Analyst level or near experience in the infrastructure sector is mandatory. Candidates must clearly demonstrate:

Hands-on experience across the infrastructure investment lifecycle, including transactions, due diligence, valuation and asset management;
Demonstrable expertise in core infrastructure sectors such as digital infrastructure, energy transition, transport & logistics, utilities or infrastructure-adjacent assets;
Proven experience in investment banking, infrastructure funds management, institutional investing or related advisory roles;
Advanced financial modelling and valuation skills, with experience leading modelling workstreams;
Strong ability to prepare and present investment recommendations to senior stakeholders and committees;
Experience contributing to portfolio monitoring, performance analysis and reporting;
Well-developed written and verbal communication skills, with strong attention to detail;
The capability to work collaboratively and mentor junior analysts within a high-performance investment team.

Experience outside the infrastructure sector alone will not be sufficient for this role; candidates must have practical infrastructure investment experience.

Life at AustralianSuper

AustralianSuper is committed to colleague development, and we support our people with ongoing learning, coaching and training, as well as career opportunities across our expanding global organisation. We offer generous leave entitlements and promote a blended working environment in which all roles can flex, and we're happy to discuss what this looks like for you.

We cultivate a workplace that champions safety, respect, inclusiveness and diversity. We are committed to supporting our diverse workforce in a way that is inclusive and embraces diversity in all its forms. If you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process or the role, please let our recruitment team know.

What's Next

Robert Half Technology are working exclusively with a leading Australian financial services organisation undergoing a major data transformation program. This is a fantastic opportunity to play a key role in modernising a large-scale enterprise data environment.

Migrating legacy SQL Server data warehouse workloads to the cloud

Supporting end-to-end data platform modernisation and optimisation

Strong experience with SQL Server data warehousing
Hands-on background with SSIS and SSAS
Experience with Databricks (PySpark preferred)
Azure Data Factory (ADF) exposure
Proven experience in cloud data migration projects
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills

At Rabobank, our commitment to our employees and clients is at the heart of everything we do.

As the world’s leading specialist in food and agribusiness banking, one of our key strengths lies in our people who have a deep understanding of agriculture and are committed to adding long-term value for clients.

Our culture is unique, because every day our people work knowing that we are all playing our part in supporting our farmers to feed the world. We strive to create an open, balanced, and flexible workplace where people feel that they belong, can be open and honest, and are supported. We actively encourage collaboration, knowledge sharing, and continuous development, and we are focused on growing each other.

About the role:

RaboResearch is Rabobank’s global knowledge and strategic insights provider. Our global and regional network of analysts are trusted sources of insights for clients, colleagues, policymakers, the industry, and the media -known for independent insights, rigorous analysis, and clear communication on the forces shaping food and agriculture.

The RaboResearch Australia & New Zealand team is looking for a Commodity Analyst to cover some of today’s most dynamic and fast‑moving parts of the agricultural value chain. This role is designed for someone who enjoys learning, collaborating, and contributing ideas in a supportive, high‑impact environment. This is an opportunity to make a difference using your analytical and communication skills and your interest in the food and agribusiness sector.

Based preferably in Sydney (Melbourne or Brisbane might be considered), this role focuses on farm inputs, including fertilisers, agrochemicals, farmland values, and farm machinery—as well as fibres, with a particular emphasis on cotton and wool. These sectors sit at the centre of major structural change, driving price volatility and technology adoption (including AI), while being heavily influenced by geopolitics, climate risk, and evolving global demand.

This is a crucial and influential role supporting the bank and its rural and corporate clients in their short-and long-term strategic decision making. We are looking for a candidate who enjoys combining data, economics, and industry knowledge—and who is equally comfortable writing, presenting, and speaking publicly.

The Analyst will be required to:

Deliver thought provoking, high‑quality market and industry research on (fertiliser/farmland prices/agrochemicals) and fibres (cotton/wool) for internal stakeholders and external clients across Australia and New Zealand

Analyse key drivers such as price volatility, cost structures, technology adoption, regulation, sustainability, and global trade flows

Contribute actively to RaboResearch’s global coverage team, partnering closely with colleagues across the world to produce consistently strong global insights and utilise those locally.

Produce engaging research outputs, including thought‑leadership reports, podcasts, presentations, videos, and digital content, with support from colleagues and established processes.

Act as the sector expert, building credibility and confidence when presenting insights at client events, industry conferences, field days, and internal strategy sessions across Australia and New Zealand

Build a public profile as a trusted voice at a pace that suits your experience and career stage, engaging with print, radio, TV, and digital media on key market and industry developments.

Work closely with Rabobank’s rural and corporate banking teams to support client conversations, identify commercial opportunities, and inform strategic thinking.

Provide research input into credit policy, sector strategy, and risk assessment, including presentations to senior management when required.

You will be encouraged to take ownership of your research while being well supported by a collaborative, trans‑Tasman and global team that covers food and agri research across crops, livestock, and consumer foods. You will benefit from joining a diverse, respectful, and inclusive team of analysts.

To be successful in the role, you will have: 

Bachelor’s Degree in related field required.

Deep industry knowledge, data literacy and technical skills, storytelling and communication and commercial mindset and ability to work across functions/geographies.

A strong analytical mindset and genuine curiosity about how agricultural markets work

Solid understanding of agriculture, agribusiness, or commodity markets gained through professional, academic, or lived experience—ideally with exposure to farm inputs (fertiliser/farmland prices/agrochemicals) and/or fibres (cotton and wool)

Confidence working with large data sets, building models, and interpreting quantitative and qualitative information, with the willingness to keep learning and ask questions.

The ability to translate complex analysis and results in clear messages and a memorable story that engage a wide range of audiences - from conferences and board rooms to kitchen tables on farm.

Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability (or ambition) to present to diverse audiences and engage with media.

A client-focused and commercial mindset and the ability to take the perspective of the client and analyze the forces that shape the future of their business.

High proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint, and comfort using new technologies and data tools to enhance analysis.

A strong collaborative mindset and willingness to connect the dots by leveraging our global and local network.

Flexibility to travel regularly and represent RaboResearch at client and industry events.

A desire to keep learning and to use technology—including emerging analytical tools—as a competitive advantage.

A positive, open mindset and the confidence to challenge established thinking in a constructive and respectful way, and to deliver clear forward-looking messages and scenarios in times of high uncertainty.

This role is well‑suited for experienced commodity analysts. Equally, this could also be an opportunity for early‑career agricultural economists and data‑driven graduates with a farming background who are eager to deepen their sector knowledge and build a public profile.

If you are early in your career, you will benefit from:

Working alongside experienced RaboResearch analysts in Australia and across our global network

Hands‑on exposure to real‑world market analysis that directly informs client decisions.

Opportunities to develop your writing, presentation, and media engagement skills.

Ongoing coaching, mentorship, feedback and support from local and global peers

We value potential, curiosity, analytical thinking, and the right attitude as much as prior experience. If you’re excited by this role and you are motivation to keep learning, we encourage you to apply even if you don’t meet every requirement listed.

What we're proud to offer you: 

Rabo@Anywhere is our hybrid way of working at Rabobank and for Australia, that means we have a blend of time working between the office and home.  

Wellbeing leave. These leave days support greater work/life balance and is just another way we are helping our employees to lead happier, healthier, and more fulfilling lives. 

Education Assistance Program. Rabobank values the development of its people and has a great Education Assistance Program to assist with professional development. 

Parental Leave that supports you and your family while giving you the freedom and flexibility to enjoy this special time (up-to 14 weeks paid leave) 

Bonus and Additional Leave. We realise sometimes 4 weeks Annual Leave isn't enough! At Rabobank we provide an incentive for eligible employees to receive 1 extra week Annual Leave and an option to purchase another 2 weeks Annual Leave. 

Extra Insurance. Rabobank recognises that employees need to protect their financial wellbeing, in the event of serious illness, injury, or even death. Rabobank provides eligible permanent employees with employer-funded Death, TPD and Income Protection Insurance. 

Rabo Workplace Giving program. As part of Rabobank's global Corporate Social Responsibility, Workplace Giving aims to encourage employees to contribute to our community. Rabo Workplace Giving program matches employee donations to selected Social Partners.

Our Values: 

Rabobank Australia values inclusion, belonging, and positive experiences for all. Our work environment, our benefits, and the way we live our values, “Client Driven”, “Responsible”, “Professional” and “Cooperative” make it a great place to work. We welcome applicants from diverse backgrounds. 

Please let our Talent Acquisition team know if you need any accommodations to make our opportunities more accessible to you. 

Rabobank is not accepting submissions from recruitment agencies for this role. 

Applicants must hold valid work rights for this role.

Ad Text:
ABOUT GM³

GM³ is an emerging Australian metallurgical coal mining entity owned by global investment entity Golden Energy and Resources (GEAR) and M Resources, a market leader in metallurgical coal industry services.

We operate two underground metallurgical coal mines, two preparation plants and a logistics chain.  Positioned for growth and focused on the future, GM³aims to contribute to the regions surrounding its operations and leverage the capability and experience of its people.

WHAT GM³ OFFERS

Options for additional superannuation contributions 

Annual short-term incentive bonus that recognises both your individual performance and the overall success of the business.

ABOUT THE ROLE

This position will be offered on a full-time, permanent contract based at our Regional Operations Centre, Port Kembla Coal Terminal in the coastal city of Wollongong, NSW, approximately 75 kilometres southwest of Sydney.

This is a hands-on coordination role supporting the delivery of technology initiatives across a dynamic operational environment. You will work closely with project teams, operational stakeholders and vendors to keep work organised, communication clear and delivery outcomes on track. The role plays an important part in ensuring technology changes are implemented smoothly and transitioned effectively into day-to-day operations.

Delivery Coordination

Support delivery of technology initiatives and operational change activities

Coordinate teams, vendors and stakeholders to keep work on track

Track timelines, risks, dependencies and key actions

Assist with planning, scheduling and requirements gathering

Operational Change & Service Integration

Support transition of solutions into BAU support

Assist with ServiceNow change, incident and request processes

Help ensure changes are implemented in a controlled and supportable way

Stakeholder Engagement & Communication

Coordinate communication between Digital Technology and business stakeholders

Prepare updates, change notices and delivery communications

Support workshops, meetings and follow-up actions

Governance, Risk & Vendor Support

Support governance, security and change management processes

Track risks, issues and dependencies

Coordinate delivery activities with vendors and internal teams

General Support & Enablement

Maintain delivery documentation and reporting

Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives

ABOUT YOU

You are organised, proactive and comfortable working across multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. You communicate confidently with both technical and non-technical stakeholders and enjoy helping teams stay aligned and delivery-focused.

You will bring:

Experience in a technology delivery, project coordination or analyst role

Strong organisation, attention to detail and follow-through

Ability to produce clear, structured documentation and maintain accurate records

Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills

Ability to manage competing priorities across multiple teams

A practical, solutions-focused approach

Desirable:

Exposure to ServiceNow or IT service management processes

Experience supporting projects or operational change initiatives

Experience in industrial, mining or multi-site environments

If you enjoy bringing people, technology and delivery together, apply now and help support impactful technology outcomes across a growing business.

GM³ is committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workforce reflective of the communities where we operate. We are proud to offer a recruitment process that is fair, accessible, and equitable for all. We strongly encourage applications from people of all backgrounds.

This job advert will close on 24 May

Applications will be assessed as they are received, and this advertisement may close early if a suitable candidate pool is identified.

Are you ready to be part of an organisation that values expertise, passion and diversity? At CFS we know that the foundation of our success lies in our exceptional people. We believe in celebrating individuality, have a passion for high performance and creating an environment where you can unleash your full potential. Our people enable us to make a difference and deliver exceptional experiences to help our customers achieve financial freedom.



Your Team

The Customer Analytics team is part of Group Data and Analytics which is responsible for partnering across CFS to deliver customer and business value from data. We leverage modern AI-enabled platforms to make insight-lead decision making a daily habit across CFS.



Your Responsibilities


Partner with stakeholders to drive tangible business outcomes - in this role your focus will be to drive impact, not just data provisioning and visualisation. This person will need to build strong relationships across business.to understand needs that make the business grow.
Own reporting products end-to-end - accountable for ~30 dashboards serving ~250 regular users; run BAU support (monitor, enhance, resolve) and deliver new dashboards aligned to business priorities.
Build dashboards and AI agents side-by-side - design, develop and iterate Databricks Genies alongside Power BI to lift self-service, speed to insight and decision quality, with strong governance and usability.
Drive disciplined, AI-enabled delivery - run an efficient BAU cadence and embed AI in design and build to materially lift speed, quality and efficiency. Prioritise simple, intentional solutions that scale.
Protect data quality and trust - maintain robust data quality checks, issue management, and clear lineage/documentation in partnership with engineering and SMEs.
Lift team capability and culture - role model ownership, curiosity and pace; contribute to Agile ceremonies and broader analytics initiatives.



Your Capability and Experience

Proven end-to-end reporting product leadership - experience owning dashboards from problem definition through to build, deployment, adoption and ongoing improvement in a BAU environment.
Strong technical foundations - strong capability in data transformation and visualisation (e.g., SQL + Power BI or similar) with a practical mindset for performance, maintainability and reusable design patterns.
Experience partnering with senior leaders - confident communicator who simplifies complexity; able to influence, align priorities, and make recommendations with senior stakeholders while maintaining trusted partnerships.
Quality-first mindset - demonstrated experience operating reliable reporting, including monitoring, data quality controls, issue triage, and continuous uplift.
Growth mindset & continuous improvement - curious, forward-looking and willing to challenge the status quo; actively seeks better ways to deliver value more efficiently.




What to Expect

At CFS, you'll be working among the very best in the wealth management industry. It's an inspiring environment that encourages development and celebrates success. Other things to look forward to:

Additional day for your birthday
Hybrid working model
Access to CFS Employer Super
Life Leave - 3 days per annum
Access to Sonder who provides human-powered medical, mental health and safety support through one easy-to-use app enabling you to get support whenever and wherever you need.
Access to Corporate rates with BUPA Health Insurance
Confidential coaching and counselling services supporting life, mind, body, relationships, work and family to assist you in taking charge of your own health and wellbeing


CFS Culture

At CFS we are committed to creating a thriving environment where individuals can flourish. We believe that success is built upon strong teams, and we are dedicated to celebrating uniqueness, championing individuality and supporting a diverse and inclusive workforce. We believe that when you can truly be yourself, you can unlock your full potential.

Apply today and join us in helping Australians to achieve their financial freedom.

Please note, CFS requires all candidates to have full work rights in Australia.

Where we have preferred candidates, background checks (including Police, Employment, Bankruptcy checks, ASIC banned and disqualified persons) will be completed prior to the final preferred candidate's employment being confirmed. The outcomes of the background checks do not preclude the preferred candidate, however, they will be assessed against the inherent requirements of the role.

Ad Text:
About Us 

Established in 1946, Teys Australia is a beef processing and food production business. We are best known for providing quality Aussie beef to customers here in Australia and around the world. 

With operations across 14 locations in Qld, NSW, Victoria, and South Australia, we are the second largest beef processor and exporter in Australia, employing over 5,000 passionate people who drive our success, and bring their very best to work every day. 

Together, we focus on feeding people and enriching lives. Join us! 

About the Role

We are seeking an experienced and forward-thinking Rewards & People Insights Managerto help to elevate our remuneration governance and workforce reporting capability across the Teys Group.

Reporting to te Enterprise General Manager People & Culture, this role plays a pivotal part in remuneration governance, ensuring market competitiveness and driving evidence‑based workforce analytics that support informed, enterprise‑wide decision making.

This is a unique opportunity for a commercially savvy remuneration and dataspecialist who thrives working in cross-functional teams and excels in a collaborative environment.

Key Responsibilities:

Lead remuneration governance, job evaluation, benchmarking and annual review cycles. 

Delver high‑quality workforce analytics, dashboards and predictive insights. 

Partner with HR, Finance and Operations to support workforce modelling on costs and inform decisions.
Provide clear reward advice, reporting and data integrity oversight across the enterprise.


About You

Essential:

3–5+ years’ experience in remuneration, reward, workforce analytics or commercial HR. 
Strong analytical capability with advanced Excel and data modelling skills (Tableau experience highly regarded).
Experienceusing Chris21(payroll), UKG, and Tableau reports

Experience with Korn Ferry (Hay Group) evaluations and salary benchmarking. 

Confidence navigating enterprise agreements, awards or industrial frameworks. 

Experience in blue‑collar, manufacturing or FMCG business highly regarded. 

Commercial acumen, strong judgement and absolute discretion with sensitive data.


What We Offer

At Teys, we believe great leadership should be supported by meaningful rewards and a workplace where you can thrive. As part of our team, you will enjoy:

Competitive remuneration package + performance incentives
Unique annual meat allowance 

Salary packaging options including devices and novated leasing 

Health, wellbeing and continuity support 

Injury/illness salary continuance scheme

Access to corporate discounts including Bupa healthcare 

Genuine investment in your development and a clear pathway to future growth

To Apply

If you are looking for an opportunity to work in a fast-paced, collaborative environment within a growing business offering excellent career development and opportunities, we encourage you to APPLY today.  

For a confidential discussion about this role, Luisa Hamze on 07 •••• •196.

You can check us out at www.teysgroup.com.au or join us on https://www.facebook.com/TeysAustralia.

The University of Melbourne

Be skilled in communicating complex data engineering concepts to diverse stakeholders.

Position Description:

JR-008545 - Senior Data Engineer - UOM 9 PD.pdf

JR-008545 - Senior Data Engineer - UOM 9 PD.docx

Drive best practice BI reporting across Retail Finance
Identify inefficiencies and recommend data-driven solutions
Build and optimise data models for reporting and analytics

Alinta Energy is one of Australia’s biggest energy retailers, generators and developers with over 1.1 million customers. Australia’s energy system is undergoing the biggest transformation in its history and we’re right there with it. We’re investing in and developing large-scale renewables and storage projects as part of our ongoing effort to provide our customers with reliable, affordable and sustainable energy.

We understand that flexibility matters when considering your next role. Let us know your requirements, and we’ll carefully consider how we might accommodate them.

Ready to make a real impact?

Join our Retail Finance team as a Snr Business Intelligence Specialist where you’ll lead the development of dashboards, reporting frameworks and data models, while driving automation and supporting data-led decision making across the business.

Why you’ll love working here

At Alinta Energy, we’re taking real action to deliver reliable and sustainable energy for Australia — and that practical approach runs through everything we do. You’ll get clear direction, genuine flexibility, and the support you need to grow, all while working on projects that make a lasting difference. We’re big enough to create impact, but small enough that your ideas and effort will always be seen.

There are plenty of other great benefits of working for Alinta Energy, including flexible working, energy discounts, an Employee Assistance Program (EAP), a paid volunteer day each year, fully stocked kitchens, and lots more.

What you’ll do

Develop end-to-end dashboards from requirements through to deployment
Build scalable Power BI reporting frameworks and enhance self-service analytics
Identify inefficient processes and recommend data-driven solutions
Automate repetitive reporting processes with particular focus around month-end
Collaborating with stakeholders to translate business needs into technical solutions
Evaluate and implement new BI tools and technologies in collaboration with the Group IT function

Your experience

Extensive experience with Power BI and SQL
Experience with Python or similar scripting language for data transformation
Skilled in data/semantic modelling for reporting
Experience driving process improvement and automation initiatives
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication capabilities
Resilient and demonstrated ability to operate effectively in a complex and changing environment

Please note this role requires full working rights within Australia.

This role is best suited to a proactive and driven individual who thrives in a complex, fast-paced environment and is passionate about delivering data-led insights.

Like what you’re seeing?

If you like the sound of this, and you’ve got experience in a similar role, we’d love to talk to you. Come and discover better together with Alinta Energy.

We are a 2026 Circle Back Initiative Employer – we commit to respond to every applicant.

If your application progresses, we will need to complete background and pre-employment checks, such as immigration checks, reference checks, criminal background checks, qualification checks, and additional checks for roles related to finance.

Our Diversity Commitment

Alinta Energy offers a truly diverse, safe and supportive work environment. As an equal opportunity employer, Alinta Energy encourages applications from any qualified person, including First Nations Australians, members of the LGBTQIA+ community, people of all ages and people living with a disability. We are also proud to be a WORK180 Endorsed Employer and we are committed to advancing women’s careers.

We look forward to hearing from you.

The purpose of this role is to lead the analysis, documentation and translation of complex business and system requirements to support the procurement and implementation of Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES) and Quality Management Systems (QMS) for the Queensland Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Service Future State Project.

 

What you'll do

In this role you will lead business analysis activities across a complex transformation project, gather and document detailed business and technical requirements, develop process maps and functional specifications, support procurement and vendor engagement activities, contribute to future-state operating models and service design, provide expert advice to stakeholders and project teams, and support the integration of manufacturing and quality systems within the Queensland Health enterprise environment.

 

About you

We are searching for someone who:

Has significant experience in business analysis within complex projects
Has experience with MES, QMS or enterprise system implementations
Can analyse and translate complex business requirements into solutions
Has strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Has experience working across transformation or digital projects
Has knowledge of healthcare, manufacturing or GMP environments (desirable)

 

Why work with us?

Work alongside passionate professionals in a supportive and inclusive environment that values people and prioritises employee success and wellbeing. This includes

Competitive salaries

12.75% superannuation
17.5% leave loading

Employee wellbeing

36.25 hour full time work week
Access to 24/7 confidential employee support providers and counsellors including for immediate family members
Additional flexibility to support your work life balance including access to generous leave entitlements, such as purchased leave, domestic violence leave, reproductive health leave, cultural leave, study and examination leave.

Professional development

Career progression with more than 300 diverse career pathways and career support to take you further!
Access to a variety of programs and initiatives to support training and career growth.
Grow your skills through hands-on experience and access to internal training opportunities and additional financial and leave benefits for approved external training and development.

 

Our commitment to equity, diversity and inclusion 

At Queensland Health, our work environment is inclusive and supportive, and we value our employees. We are an equal opportunity employer and encourage applications from people of all cultures, abilities and backgrounds.

 

Our commitment to cultural safety, equity, diversity and inclusion means we understand some people may need changes to the recruitment process. If you need support during the recruitment process, such as meeting with the panel virtually instead of in person, please reach out to the hiring manager. We value diverse candidates and your need for adjustments will not affect our hiring decisions.
